question.text: By what date each year must Indian companies that have received FDI file the annual return on Foreign Liabilities and Assets (FLA)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** All Indian companies which have received FDI and/or made FDI abroad in the previous year(s) including the current year, should file the annual return on Foreign Liabilities and Assets (FLA) in the soft form to the Reserve Bank, Department of Statistics and Information Management, Mumbai by July 15 every year. **The Trap:** The FLA return is an annual filing with a specific date (July 15), not aligned with the financial year-end (March 31) or calendar year-end. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The FLA return covers both inward and outward FDI and is filed with RBI's Department of Statistics, not the banking department that handles regular FDI reporting.
